# ARC-QUIC-CRT-0028 - QUIC CRT detached resumption-ticket carrier handoff architecture

## Purpose

Describe the smallest honest cross-lifetime ticket handoff seam: export an opaque detached carrier from the owned snapshot on the managed client/runtime path and store it as dormant internal state in a later client setup without changing handshake behavior or public surface.

## Scope

This slice covers only detached export and dormant import of opaque resumption-ticket state across client connection lifetimes. It does not add PSK derivation, a ClientHello replay path, 0-RTT packet support, anti-replay, key update, or a public API expansion.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0130

## Design Summary

The managed client/runtime path already owns a runtime-local opaque resumption-ticket snapshot under REQ-QUIC-CRT-0127, and the bridge/runtime path already surfaces ticket ingress under REQ-QUIC-CRT-0128 and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0129. This slice adds a separate immutable detached carrier that the originating runtime can export by value and that a later managed client setup can accept as dormant internal state. The carrier remains opaque and does not participate in handshake behavior, early-data admission, PSK derivation, or any public surface change.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicDetachedResumptionTicketSnapshot.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicClientConnectionOptionsValidator.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicClientConnectionHost.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnection.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/QuicPostHandshakeTicketTestSupport.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0130.cs

## Risks

- If the detached carrier is reused as PSK material or fed into ClientHello, the slice will overgrow into resumption infrastructure the repository has not proven.
- If later client setup stores the carrier only as a renamed alias for the existing runtime-owned bytes, the slice will not be a real cross-lifetime handoff.
- If the carrier changes handshake/bootstrap behavior or opens early data, the slice will overclaim capability it still does not own.
